topics.title: Getting Started with Markdown
topics.body:
## Getting Started

Markdown is a lightweight markup language that you can use to format text. Created by John Gruber in 2004, it's designed to be easy to read and write in plain text, then converted to HTML.

### Why Markdown?
- **Readable:** The source looks like plain text — no angle brackets or complex syntax.
- **Portable:** `.md` files open in any text editor.
- **Versatile:** Used for READMEs, documentation, notes, blog posts, and more.

### Basic Principles
1. Write in plain text with simple punctuation for formatting.
2. A Markdown processor converts it to HTML or rich text.
3. Sputnik uses **CommonMark** with **GFM extensions** (tables, strikethrough, task lists).

### Your First Markdown File
Create a `.md` file and start typing. Use `#` for headings, `**` for bold, and blank lines to separate paragraphs.

> **Tip:** Open the Markdown Preview panel (`View > Markdown Preview`) to see your rendered output live as you edit.
topics.exampleCode:
# My First Markdown Document

Hello, **world**! This is *Markdown*.

- Easy to write
- Easy to read
- Converts to HTML

topics.title: Headings
topics.body:
## Headings

Headings create a document hierarchy and improve readability. Markdown supports six heading levels, matching HTML's `<h1>` through `<h6>`.

### Syntax
```markdown
# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3
#### Heading 4
##### Heading 5
###### Heading 6
```

### Best Practices
- Use **one `# Heading 1`** per document (the title).
- Nest headings logically: don't jump from `##` to `####`.
- Headings appear in the Markdown Preview's outline/table of contents.

### Alternative Syntax
Headings 1 and 2 can also be written with underlines:
```
Heading 1
=========

Heading 2
---------
```

> **Sputnik tip:** Right-click any heading in the editor and select "Look Up Help" to jump here instantly.
topics.exampleCode:
# Document Title

## Section One
Content here...

### Sub-section
More detailed content.

topics.title: Bold & Italic
topics.body:
## Bold & Italic

Emphasise text with bold and italic formatting. Markdown offers multiple syntaxes for each.

### Italic
Wrap text in single asterisks or underscores:
```markdown
*italic text*  or  _italic text_
```

### Bold
Wrap text in double asterisks or underscores:
```markdown
**bold text**  or  __bold text__
```

### Bold + Italic
Combine three asterisks or underscores:
```markdown
***bold and italic***  or  ___bold and italic___
```

### Rules
- Use the **same delimiter** to open and close (don't mix `*` and `_` in the same span).
- Delimiters **must touch** the enclosed text (no space after opening `*`).
- Mid-word emphasis is supported: `un**believable**` → un**believable**.

> **Common mistake:** `* text*` won't render as italic because of the space after `*`.
topics.exampleCode:
This is **important** and this is *subtle*.

You can also use ___both together___ for
maximum emphasis.

topics.title: Links
topics.body:
## Links

Markdown supports inline links, reference-style links, and autolinks.

### Inline Links
```markdown
[link text](https://example.com "Optional title")
```
The title attribute (hover tooltip) is optional.

### Reference-Style Links
Useful for documents with many links — keep the source clean:
```markdown
[link text][ref-id]

[ref-id]: https://example.com "Optional title"
```

### Autolinks
Wrap a URL or email in angle brackets:
```markdown
<https://example.com>
<user@example.com>
```

### URLs with Special Characters
Encode spaces and parentheses: `https://example.com/page%20name`.

> **GFM extension:** URLs typed directly in text (without brackets) are auto-linked in GFM.
topics.exampleCode:
Visit [Sputnik](https://sputnik.app) for more info.

Email us at <hello@sputnik.app>.

[See the docs][docs]

[docs]: https://docs.sputnik.app

topics.title: Images
topics.body: ## Images Images are like links but with a leading `!`. ### Inline Images ```markdown ![alt text](path/to/image.png "Optional title") ``` ### Reference-Style Images ```markdown ![alt text][img-ref] [img-ref]: path/to/image.png ``` ### Image Dimensions Standard Markdown doesn't support width/height. Use HTML for sizing: ```html ``` > **Sputnik tip:** Drag an image from the Project File Tree into the editor to insert it with the correct relative path.
topics.exampleCode:
![Sputnik logo](logo.png)

See the screenshot below:

![App screenshot](screenshots/main.png)

topics.title: Lists
topics.body:
## Lists

### Unordered Lists
Use `-`, `*`, or `+` followed by a space:
```markdown
- Item one
- Item two
  - Nested item
```

### Ordered Lists
Use numbers followed by a period:
```markdown
1. First step
2. Second step
   1. Sub-step
```

### Nested Lists
Indent by **4 spaces** or **1 tab** to nest.

### Task Lists (GFM)
```markdown
- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Pending task
- [ ] Another task
```

> **Tip:** Mix list markers to distinguish levels: use `-` for top level, `*` for second level.
topics.exampleCode:
## Shopping List

- Fruits
  - Apples
  - Bananas
- Vegetables
  - Carrots
  - Broccoli

## Steps

1. Unpack
2. Assemble
3. Enjoy

topics.title: Inline Code & Code Blocks
topics.body:
## Inline Code & Code Blocks

### Inline Code
Wrap text in single backticks:
```markdown
Use the `print()` function in Python.
```

To display a literal backtick inside code, use double backticks:
```markdown
`` Use backticks: `code` here ``
```

### Fenced Code Blocks
Use triple backticks with an optional language tag:
````markdown
```swift
func greet(name: String) {
    print("Hello, \(name)!")
}
```
````

### Indented Code Blocks
Indent each line by 4 spaces or 1 tab:
```
    This is a code block.
    No backticks needed.
```

> **Sputnik tip:** Code blocks with a language tag get syntax highlighting in the Markdown Preview.
topics.exampleCode:
Here's some inline `code` in a sentence.

```python
def fibonacci(n):
    a, b = 0, 1
    for _ in range(n):
        yield a
        a, b = b, a + b
```

topics.title: Tables
topics.body:
## Tables (GFM)

Tables are a GFM extension. They use pipes (`|`) and hyphens (`-`) to define columns and alignment.

### Basic Table
```markdown
| Name  | Role       |
|-------|-----------|
| Ada   | Engineer   |
| Bo    | Designer   |
```

### Column Alignment
Control alignment with colons in the separator row:
```markdown
| Left   | Center | Right  |
|:-------|:------:|-------:|
| Text   | Text   | 123    |
```

### Rules
- Pipe characters at the outer edges are **optional**.
- Each row must have the same number of cells.
- Use `\|` to include a literal pipe in a cell.
- The header row and separator row are **required**.

> **Sputnik tip:** Copy a table from Numbers or Excel and paste it into the editor — Sputnik auto-formats it as a Markdown table.
topics.exampleCode:
| Feature     | Supported | Notes         |
|------------|:---------:|--------------:|
| Tables      | ✅        | GFM extension |
| Alignment   | ✅        | Left/Center/Right |
| Nesting     | ❌        | Not supported |

topics.title: Blockquotes
topics.body:
## Blockquotes

Blockquotes indent and style quoted text. Use `>` at the start of each line.

### Single Paragraph
```markdown
> This is a blockquote.
```

### Multi-Paragraph
Add `>` on blank lines between paragraphs:
```markdown
> First paragraph.
>
> Second paragraph.
```

### Nested Blockquotes
Use multiple `>` characters:
```markdown
> Outer quote
>> Nested quote
>>> Deeply nested
```

### Blockquotes with Other Elements
Blockquotes can contain headings, lists, and code:
```markdown
> ## Quoted Heading
>
> - List item
> - Another item
>
> ```
> code inside quote
> ```
```

> **Tip:** Blockquotes are great for callouts, testimonials, and quoting external sources.
topics.exampleCode:
> The best way to predict the
> future is to invent it.
>
> — Alan Kay

topics.title: Horizontal Rules
topics.body:
## Horizontal Rules

Create a thematic break with three or more of the same character on a line.

### Syntax
Any of these produce a `<hr>`:
```markdown
---
***
___
```

You can also use spaces between characters:
```markdown
* * *
- - -
_ _ _
```

### Rules
- Must be on their **own line**.
- Must have **3 or more** characters.
- Blank lines before and after are optional but recommended for readability.

### Common Pitfall
A line of hyphens directly under text is interpreted as a **Setext heading** (level 2), not a horizontal rule:
```
This is a heading
-----------------
```
To prevent this, add a blank line above the hyphens.
topics.exampleCode:
Section One

---

Section Two

***

Section Three

topics.title: GFM Extensions
topics.body:
## GitHub Flavored Markdown (GFM) Extensions

Sputnik supports GFM extensions on top of CommonMark:

### Strikethrough
```markdown
~~deleted text~~
```

### Tables
```markdown
| Col A | Col B |
|-------|-------|
| Val 1 | Val 2 |
```

### Task Lists
```markdown
- [x] Done
- [ ] To-do
```

### Autolinks
URLs typed directly become clickable links.

### Footnotes (selected variants)
```markdown
Sentence with a footnote.[^1]

[^1]: The footnote content.
```

### Disallowed Raw HTML
Sputnik strips raw HTML for security in the Markdown Preview — use only Markdown syntax.
topics.exampleCode:
### GFM in Action

~~Outdated info~~

- [x] Implement login
- [ ] Add tests
- [ ] Write docs

Visit https://sputnik.app

This is auto-linked in GFM.
